sprint: Page Background Colour vs. a Scene-Hosted Background Object
task: 'i changed BG color it did not worked' -- a real 'My shapes' World screenshot showed Object Strip's real, editable 'Background' card, yet Personalize's own Background Colour swatch had zero visible effect. Root cause: the earlier per-page-override fix genuinely wins over the World's wall tone in the BASE canvas fill, but a World-authored, Scene-hosted 'Background' object (Builder's 'Hosted by Scene' Colour Experience, target:'slide') paints AFTER that base fill as part of the merged Scene Stack render every zero-Holder Scene now uses -- so the World's own full-bleed fill always painted right over the swatch, silently. Fix: js/contextPanel.js's _appendBackground gained _sceneHostedBackgroundObject(), scanning the render tree for owner:'world'+target:'slide'+visual.kind:'color' (the exact fingerprint a Scene-hosted Colour fill leaves); when found, the swatch reads/writes that object's real colour via SceneEngine.setContentOverride -- the identical mechanism Object Strip's own edit control already uses, so both entry points agree. Non-editable shows an honest note instead of a dead swatch. No Scene-hosted object at all (plain wall-tone pages) keeps the exact original per-page-override behaviour unchanged. Verified via a new Playwright reproduction (background_color_bug_test2.js) matching the reported shape exactly: canvas shows the real World colour before edit, swatch opens showing that real value (not a stale default), editing updates both model and canvas, Object Strip's own control agrees. The original fix's own regression test (no Scene-hosted object) still passes unchanged. Full regression across the existing Creator/Builder suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero app console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change.
